tikz-curvilinearSphericalCylindrical-01-: Two side-by-side 3D sketches on x, y, z axes for cylindrical coordinates: the left shows a cylinder of radius r with a horizontal cross-section circle at height z; the right adds a red point (x,y,z) on the cylinder and the angle theta from the positive x-axis.
tikz-curvilinearSphericalCylindrical-02-: Two side-by-side 3D sketches for spherical coordinates: the left shows a sphere of radius rho about the origin; the right shows the same sphere with a red latitude circle of constant phi, where phi is measured from the positive z-axis.
tikz-curvilinearSphericalCylindrical-03-: Three-dimensional axis frame (x, y, z) with a red ray of length rho from the origin to a point (x,y,z), the angle phi from the positive z-axis to the ray, the projection (x,y,0) in the xy-plane shown in blue, and the angle theta from the positive x-axis to that projection.
tikz-curvilinearSphericalCylindrical-04-: Three-dimensional x, y, z axis frame summarizing Cartesian, cylindrical (r,theta,z), and spherical (rho,phi,theta) coordinates for one point: a red ray rho to (x,y,z) with angle phi from the z-axis, and a blue ray r to (x,y,0) with angle theta from the x-axis.
tikz-RnVectorsScalarMultMeaning-01-2ad00: Two-dimensional sketch of two vectors drawn as arrows: a blue vector u going up and to the right, and a red vector v of similar length pointing down and to the right, used as the starting figure before scalar multiples are drawn.
tikz-RnVectorsScalarMultMeaning-02-39f3c: Two-dimensional diagram showing scalar multiples of two vectors: blue vector u and its negative -u (same length, opposite direction), plus red vector v together with 2v (twice as long, same direction) and -1/2 v (half-length, reversed).
tikz-RnVectorsScalarMultMeaning-03-2ad00: Two-dimensional sketch of vectors u (blue, up and right) and v (red, down and right) drawn as separate arrows, presented as the setup for graphing the linear combinations u+2v and u-1/2 v.
tikz-RnVectorsScalarMultMeaning-04-414c5: Two-dimensional vector diagram of the linear combination u + 2v: blue vector u from the origin, red vector 2v drawn tip-to-tail starting at u's head, and the purple resultant u+2v from the origin to the final tip.
tikz-RnVectorsScalarMultMeaning-05-65fa5: Two-dimensional vector diagram of the linear combination u - 1/2 v: blue u from the origin, the short red vector -1/2 v placed tip-to-tail at u's head, and the purple resultant u - 1/2 v drawn from the origin to the final tip.
tikz-spectraltheoryOrthogonalityQuadrati: Plot of the level curve 6 x_1^2 + 4 x_1 x_2 + 3 x_2^2 = 7 in the x_1, x_2 plane: a tilted blue ellipse whose principal axes are rotated relative to the coordinate axes, illustrating the cross-term x_1 x_2.
Plot in the y_1, y_2 plane of the simplified level curve 7 y_1^2 + 2 y_2^2 = 7, drawn as an upright blue ellipse aligned with the new coordinate axes after diagonalization removes the cross term.

Plot of the level curve 5 x_1^2 - 6 x_1 x_2 + 5 x_2^2 = 8 in the x_1, x_2 plane: a tilted blue ellipse whose principal axes are rotated 45 degrees from the coordinate axes due to the x_1 x_2 cross term.
Plot in the y_1, y_2 plane of the diagonalized level curve 2 y_1^2 + 8 y_2^2 = 8, shown as a horizontally elongated blue ellipse aligned with the new principal axes.
tikz-RnVectorsParametricLines-01-a7cb033: Two-dimensional sketch of a line L through points P_0, P, and Q drawn as a blue diagonal arrow, with light grid helpers indicating the coordinates of each point, illustrating Q as an arbitrary point on the line determined by P_0 and the direction P - P_0.
tikz-lineartransformationsSpecialR2-01-b: Two-dimensional diagram of a rotation T applied to vectors u and v: cyan arrows show u, v, and their sum u+v with the dotted parallelogram completion, and blue arrows show the rotated images T(u), T(v), and T(u)+T(v), illustrating that T preserves vector addition.
tikz-lineartransformationsSpecialR2-02-e: Two-dimensional unit circle showing rotation by angle theta: cyan basis vectors e_1, e_2 on the x- and y-axes, and blue rotated images R_theta(e_1) = (cos theta, sin theta) and R_theta(e_2) = (-sin theta, cos theta), with theta marked at the origin.
tikz-RnVectorsOrthogonalityLeastSquares-: Three-dimensional schematic of orthogonal projection onto the column space A(R^n), drawn as a shaded gray plane: vector y from the origin, its projection z = A x in the plane, the red residual y - z perpendicular to the plane, and another vector u in the plane farther from y.
Two-dimensional scatter plot with axes labelled x and y showing the five data points (0,1), (1,2), (2,2), (3,4), (4,5) and the blue least-squares regression line y = x + 0.8 passing through them.
tikz-RnVectorsApplicationsPhysics-01-f12: Three-dimensional axis frame with x, y, and z axes drawn through the origin, and the three standard basis vectors e_1, e_2, e_3 (also labelled i, j, k) shown as thick red, green, and blue arrows of unit length along the coordinate axes.
tikz-RnVectorsApplicationsPhysics-02-001: Two-dimensional sketch of compass-style horizontal and vertical axes with a thick blue arrow from the origin pointing up and to the right at a 45-degree angle, representing an airplane flying northeast at 100 mph.
tikz-RnVectorsApplicationsPhysics-03-d75: Two-dimensional diagram of a swimmer crossing a river: two horizontal lines mark the riverbanks; a blue arrow of length 4 points west representing the current, and a perpendicular blue arrow of length 3 points north representing the swimmer's velocity directly across.
tikz-RnVectorsDotProductSignificance-01-: Two-dimensional sketch of two vectors emanating from a common origin: a blue vector v pointing up and to the right and a red vector u pointing down and to the right, with the included angle theta marked between them.
tikz-RnVectorsApplicationsWork-01-cc02b2: Two-dimensional vector diagram showing a force F (purple arrow) applied at point P, decomposed into the parallel component F_|| (blue) along the direction of motion PQ and the perpendicular component F_perp (red), with angle theta between F and PQ.
tikz-RnVectorsCrossProductBox-01-47fdd83: Three-dimensional sketch of a parallelepiped determined by three blue vectors u, v, w emanating from a common corner, with the cross product u x v drawn vertically upward and the angle theta shown between u x v and the edge w.
